headline: French firefighters brace for high-risk season after record heatwave
description: Fifty-two administrative departments across France are at risk of forest wildfires.

From France to Italy, inside the counterfeit rings circulating fake banknotes across Europe via social media
description: A joint French-Italian investigation that lasted over a year led to the indictment of 12 people suspected of belonging to a huge counterfeiting network.

headline: 250 years of American independence: The retreat from multiracial democracy
description: 'America 250' (7/13). A century after its founding, 38 states made up the nation. Deep divisions persisted over how to treat the Black population. Slavery had been abolished, much to the dismay of the Democrats, who were gaining ground. Although the Republicans won the presidential election, their victory came at the cost of institutionalizing segregationist policies in the Southern states.

headline: 2026 World Cup: In New York, football takes over the city where basketball is king
description: The North American World Cup could be on the brink of giving football its rightful place in New York City. Fields are popping up across the city, and an ever-growing number of players are starting to overshadow traditional American sports.

headline: To join the 'system' or overturn it? Far-right leader Bardella's private life and policies raise doubts
description: By openly seeking to appeal to economic elites and the right, far-right leader Jordan Bardella breaks with Marine Le Pen and her father's historic 'anti-system' hostility. Some party members question his desire to pursue radical policies.

Parents outraged by lack of school guidelines during heatwave: 'We've had to scramble to make childcare arrangements'
description: As classroom conditions worsen, parents have tried to adapt by working from home, taking time off or relying on other adults for help. All have criticized the authorities' lack of preparation and have expressed concern for the years ahead.

headline: Sleeping in a cellar, waiting at a funeral home, riding the tram: How the French are improvising to survive the heatwave
description: Residents describe the sometimes unusual strategies they have adopted to endure the heat. Their improvised solutions highlight the shortcomings in climate change adaptation.

headline: In the age of AI, Chinese universities overhaul their curricula
description: Universities across China are undergoing a major transformation to align with the economic priorities of the Chinese government and to tackle youth unemployment. To survive, some disciplines, such as foreign languages, now have to attach themselves to hard sciences.
